creator: Tim Fernholz
title: (No AI) Musk mulled handing OpenAI to his children, Altman testifies
dc:creator: Tim Fernholz
content: Altman said that Musk's focus on controlling the initial for-profit gave him pause because OpenAI was dedicated to keeping advanced AI out of the hands of a single person, and Altman, with his experience running the prominent startup accelerator Y Combinator, knew "founders who had control usually did not give it up."
contentSnippet: Altman said that Musk's focus on controlling the initial for-profit gave him pause because OpenAI was dedicated to keeping advanced AI out of the hands of a single person, and Altman, with his experience running the prominent startup accelerator Y Combinator, knew "founders who had control usually did not give it up."

creator: Ram Iyer
title: (No AI) Anthropic warns investors against secondary platforms offering access to its shares
dc:creator: Ram Iyer
content: "Any sale or transfer of Anthropic stock, or any interest in Anthropic stock, offered by these firms is void and will not be recognized on our books and records," the company's support page reads.
contentSnippet: "Any sale or transfer of Anthropic stock, or any interest in Anthropic stock, offered by these firms is void and will not be recognized on our books and records," the company's support page reads.

title: (No AI) Meta won’t let you block its AI account on Threads
author: Jay Peters
content:
![An image showing the Threads logo](https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/chorus/uploads/chorus_asset/file/24774110/STK156_Instagram_threads_1.jpg?quality=90&strip=all&crop=0,0,100,100)

Meta announced on Tuesday that it's testing a Threads feature that lets users tag a Meta AI account to get answers to questions or context about a conversation on the platform. If you've spent any time looking at replies on X as of late, this new feature sounds a lot like Meta's take on people tagging xAI's Grok. But, [as reported by _Engadget_](https://www.engadget.com/2171113/threads-users-are-pissed-they-cant-block-metas-new-ai-chatbot/), Threads users quickly discovered that you can't block the new Meta AI account, and they aren't happy about it.

Meta has invested heavily in AI as it works to catch up to rivals like OpenAI and Google, [spending billions to hire AI talent](https://www.theverge.com/ai-artificial-intelligence/703929/meta-openai-anthropic-superintelligence-lab-ai-poaching-money). It launched a new AI model called [Muse Spark in April](https://www.theverge.com/tech/908769/meta-muse-spark-ai-model-launch-rollout), which it s …

[Read the full story at The Verge.](https://www.theverge.com/tech/929091/meta-ai-threads-account-block)

But, as reported by Engadget, Threads users quickly discovered that you can't block the new Meta AI account, and they aren't happy about it.
Meta has invested heavily in AI as it works to catch up to rivals like OpenAI and Google, spending billions to hire AI talent. It launched a new AI model called Muse Spark in April, which it s …

title: (No AI) Sam Altman says Elon Musk’s mind games were damaging OpenAI
author: Emma Roth
content:
![Stylized image of OpenAI CEO Sam Altman, seated](https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2025/09/STK201_SAM_ALTMAN_CVIRGINIA_B.jpg?quality=90&strip=all&crop=0,0,100,100)

OpenAI CEO Sam Altman says Elon Musk did "huge damage" to the culture of the AI startup. During testimony as part of [Musk's lawsuit against OpenAI](https://www.theverge.com/tech/917225/sam-altman-elon-musk-openai-lawsuit), Altman said Musk required OpenAI president Greg Brockman and former chief scientist Ilya Sutskever to rank researchers by their accomplishments and "take a chainsaw through a bunch."

Altman conceded that [this was the management style the Tesla CEO was known for](https://www.theverge.com/23551060/elon-musk-twitter-takeover-layoffs-workplace-salute-emoji), but that it was incompatible with his startup. "I don't think Mr. Musk understood how to run a good research lab," Altman testified when his lawyer, William Savitt, asked about the impact of Musk's departure from OpenAI on morale. "For a …

[Read the full story at The Verge.](https://www.theverge.com/ai-artificial-intelligence/928861/openai-sam-altman-elon-musk-damage)

Altman conceded that this was the management style the Tesla CEO was known for, but that it was incompatible with his startup. title: (No AI) What Parameter Golf taught us about AI-assisted research
content: Parameter Golf brought together 1,000+ participants and 2,000+ submissions to explore AI-assisted machine learning research, coding agents, quantization, and novel model design under strict constraints.

title: (No AI) Ilya Sutskever Stands by His Role in Sam Altman’s OpenAI Ouster: ‘I Didn’t Want It to Be Destroyed’

content: The former OpenAI chief scientist may be estranged from the company, but he still came to its defense as he testified on Monday.
